question: What is the legal name of your business?
helperText: Provide the exact registered name of your business as per your GST/PAN registration.
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of your business as it appears on your GST certificate or PAN card.
Avoid using abbreviations or short forms unless they are part of your registered name.
Double-check the spelling and capitalization to ensure it matches your official documents.

question: What is your business registration number?
helperText: This is the unique identification number assigned to your business by the government. It is typically a 10-15 digit alphanumeric code.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Business Registration Number
guidanceTips: The business registration number is typically your Goods and Services Tax (GST) Identification Number (GSTIN) or Permanent Account Number (PAN).
The number should be entered exactly as it appears on your business registration documents, including any letters and special characters.
Ensure that you provide the correct and complete business registration number, as this information is crucial for insurance underwriting and compliance purposes.

question: What is your GST (Goods and Services Tax) registration number?
helperText: The GST number is a unique 15-digit identification number assigned to your business by the Indian government. It is mandatory for all registered businesses in India.
guidanceTitle: How to find your GST number
guidanceTips: Your GST number is printed on your GST registration certificate.
If you don't have the certificate, you can look up your GST number on the GST portal (www.gst.gov.in) using your business name or PAN (Permanent Account Number).
Make sure to enter the GST number exactly as it appears on the certificate, including the state code, PAN, and other characters.

question: What is your Permanent Account Number (PAN)?
helperText: PAN is a unique 10-digit alphanumeric number issued by the Income Tax Department. It is mandatory for all business transactions in India.
guidanceTitle: How to find and enter your PAN correctly
guidanceTips: Your PAN card contains your 10-digit PAN number. Ensure you enter it exactly as printed on the card.
PAN numbers always start with 5 uppercase letters followed by 4 digits and 1 uppercase letter.
Double-check your PAN number before submitting the form to avoid any errors or delays in processing.

question: What is the primary business activity or industry of your shop?
helperText: Select the option that best describes your primary business activity. If your business type is not listed, choose 'Other' and provide a brief description.
guidanceTips: Choose the business type that represents the majority of your shop's revenue and operations.
If your business engages in multiple activities, select the one that is the core focus.
Provide a specific description if you select 'Other' to help the insurer understand your business.

question: What is the year your business was established?
helperText: Enter the 4-digit year your business was officially registered or started operations.
guidanceTips: The year of establishment is the year your business was officially registered or started operations, not necessarily the year you started planning the business.
This information is important for the insurer to understand the longevity and experience of your business.
Ensure you enter the exact 4-digit year, as this field does not accept ranges or partial years.

question: What is the total number of employees in your business?
placeholder: Enter the exact number of employees
helperText: Please provide the current total number of employees in your business. This information is required to calculate the appropriate insurance coverage and premium.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing the Number of Employees
guidanceTips: Count all full-time, part-time, and contractual employees working in your business.
Ensure the number you provide is the exact current total, not an estimate or range.
This information is crucial for the insurance provider to assess the risk and offer the right coverage for your business.

question: What is your annual business revenue?
placeholder: Enter your annual revenue in INR
helperText: Please provide your exact annual revenue in Indian Rupees (INR). Do not include any commas or special characters.
guidanceTips: Enter your total annual sales or income generated from your shopkeeping business.
Round the figure to the nearest whole number, without any decimal places.
If your business is new and you don't have a full year of revenue data yet, provide your best estimate for the upcoming 12-month period.

question: What is the complete address of your business?
helperText: Provide the full address including street, locality, city, state, and pincode.
guidanceTitle: Tips for Providing Your Business Address
guidanceTips: Include the building number, street name, locality, landmark (if any), city, state, and 6-digit pincode.
Ensure the address matches your GST and PAN registration details.
Avoid abbreviations and use the full names of the city and state.

question: What is the city where your shop is located?
helperText: Select the city where your shop is located. This is important for determining the applicable taxes and regulations.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Filling the City Field
guidanceTips: Enter the exact city name where your shop is located. Do not use abbreviations or short forms.
If your shop is located in a suburb or a smaller town, select the nearest major city from the list.
Ensure that the city name you provide matches the address on your GST registration and other business documents.

question: In which state is your shop located?
options: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
helperText: Select the state where your shop is located.
guidanceTips: Ensure you select the correct state where your shop is registered and operating.
The state selection is important for determining the applicable taxes, regulations, and insurance requirements.
If you have multiple locations, select the state where your primary or registered shop is located.

question: What is the PIN Code of your business location?
helperText: The 6-digit PIN Code is required to determine the location and applicable insurance rates.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering the PIN Code
guidanceTips: The PIN Code is a 6-digit number that uniquely identifies the geographic area of your business location.
You can find the PIN Code for your address on India Post's website or by contacting your local post office.
Ensure you enter the PIN Code accurately, as it is used to calculate the insurance premium based on the risk profile of your location.

question: Who is the primary contact person for this insurance policy?
placeholder: Enter full name of contact person
helperText: Provide the full name of the person who will be the primary point of contact for this insurance policy.
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of the contact person, including first, middle (if applicable), and last name.
Ensure the name matches the government-issued ID (such as PAN card or Aadhaar) that will be provided for this policy.
Avoid using abbreviations or nicknames in the name field.

question: What is your business email address?
helperText: Please provide a valid email address associated with your business. This will be used for all communication regarding your insurance policy.
guidanceTitle: Entering a Valid Business Email Address
guidanceTips: Use an email address that is associated with your registered business, not a personal email.
Ensure the email address is spelled correctly and does not contain any typos.
Avoid using generic email providers like @gmail.com or @yahoo.com. Use a domain name that matches your business name.
If you don't have a dedicated business email, consider setting one up before proceeding with the insurance application.

question: What is your business's primary contact phone number?
placeholder: Enter your 10-digit mobile or landline number
helperText: Please provide a valid 10-digit phone number where you can be reached.
guidanceTips: Enter your primary business contact number, which can be a mobile or landline.
The number should be 10 digits long and start with 6, 7, 8, or 9.
Ensure the number provided is active and regularly monitored, as the insurance provider may need to contact you regarding your policy.

question: When would you like your shopkeeper's insurance policy to start?
helperText: The policy period is typically 1 year. Please select a start date within the next 12 months.
guidanceTips: Choose a start date that aligns with your business operations and renewal cycle.
The policy period is typically 1 year, so the start date should be between April 1st and March 31st.
Consider any upcoming business events, seasonal changes, or regulatory requirements that may impact the optimal policy start date.

question: When does your current policy expire?
helperText: Enter the date when your current insurance policy expires. The new policy will start from the next day.
guidanceTips: The policy end date should be within the next 12 months from today's date.
Ensure the date is accurate as it determines the start date of your new policy.
If you're unsure of the exact date, provide your best estimate based on your current policy documents.

question: What is the total value of your shop and its contents that you would like to insure?
placeholder: Enter the total value in INR
helperText: This is the total value of your shop, inventory, and equipment that you want to be covered by the insurance policy.
guidanceTips: Consider the current market value of your shop, the value of your inventory, and the cost of your equipment and fixtures.
Factor in any potential appreciation or depreciation in the value of your assets over the 1-year policy period.
Review your previous insurance policies or financial statements to get an accurate estimate of the total value to insure.
Ensure the coverage amount is sufficient to fully protect your business in case of any damages or losses.

question: Who is your current insurance provider for your shopkeepers insurance policy?
placeholder: Enter the name of your current insurance provider
helperText: Provide the name of the insurance company that currently provides your shopkeepers insurance coverage.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Current Insurance Provider
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of the insurance company, not just the brand name.
If you have multiple insurance policies, provide the information for the shopkeepers insurance policy specifically.
Ensure the spelling and capitalization matches the official name of the insurance provider.

question: Please provide details of any previous insurance claims made by your business.
placeholder: Enter details of previous claims, including claim amounts, dates, and reasons
helperText: Provide complete details of any previous insurance claims made by your business. This will help us assess the risk profile accurately.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Previous Claims History
guidanceTips: Be as detailed as possible in describing the previous claims, including the claim amounts, dates, and reasons for the claims.
Mention any claims that were denied or only partially paid, and provide the reasons for the same.
Highlight any steps taken by your business to mitigate the risks that led to the previous claims.
Ensure that the information provided is accurate and matches the records maintained by your business.

question: What risk management measures do you have in place for your shopkeeping business?
placeholder: Describe your risk management measures in detail
helperText: Provide information on safety equipment, security systems, fire prevention, and other measures taken to mitigate risks.
guidanceTips: List all the safety and security equipment installed in your shop, such as fire extinguishers, smoke detectors, CCTV cameras, burglar alarms, etc.
Explain the procedures and protocols you have in place for emergency situations like fire, theft, or natural disasters.
Describe any training programs you provide to your employees on risk management and safety practices.
